stem: A pupil is writing a program that stores: a person's age, their average exam score and whether they are a club member.
model_answer: Age is a whole number, so use an integer. The average score may have a fractional part (e.g. 73.5), so use a real / float. Membership is yes/no, so use a Boolean. Each type uses just enough memory to represent the data and avoids accidental misuse — for example, you cannot accidentally store '73.5 years old' if age is an integer.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award one mark per correct (data type + reason) pair, capped at 3.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: State the most appropriate data type for each of the three values and explain why.

mark_points.text: average score → real / float (can have a fractional part)
